text:
Once upon a time in the quiet village of Everwood, there lived a mysterious fox named Félix. People whispered about him. He loved climbing tall hickory trees during twilight.

One day, Félix decided he would climb higher than ever before. He ascended until soft shadows enveloped his senses. At the apex, he noticed something peculiar. A strange circle of light hung motionless.

Curious, Félix prowled closer. Inside was an old book. Its cover smelled of earthiness. He read verses that spoke of stars dreaming.

Feeling bold, he poked needles through parchment fibers. Dreams. He pondered them deeply.

Starlight whispered secrets. In dreams, he flew across oceans. Another time, he was silent music amidst blooms.

Back to earth. He wrote poems with moonlight. Friends took them.

Then came autumn leaves. A dream starfall.

Felix climbed more. Dreams grew grander. Owls greeted him.

Stars whispered tales. He learned
